rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION, FLUOR INTERNATIONAL, INC, FLUOR INTERCONTINENTAL, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. While plaintiff's failed to provide an opposing separate statement is sufficient grounds to grant the motion, the motion is granted on its merits. Dfendant satisfied its burden by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses (plaintiff's response to special interrogatory 34) showing that plaintiff does not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to establish the cause of the helicopter crash. Plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue whether Tryco's misfeasance was a substantial factor in causing the accident. The res ipsa loquitor doctrine is inapplicable. Plaintiff has not shown that the accident is of a kind that does not ordinarily occur without a negligent act. The crash occurred in a warzone. Moreover, plaintiff has not shown that the instrumentality which caused the accident was under the exclusive control of the defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 3, plaintiff ann huang's Motion To Compel Defendant'S Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The motion is unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN WU VS. DEAN SENIFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And Request For Monetary Sanctions And Penalty
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT DEAN SENIFF Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment And Request For Monetary Sanctions And Penalty. Granted. Plaintiff's counsel is required to pay $980 to defendant no later than June 15, 2012. In the context of this case, the interest shortfall of $17.17 is a trifle. Civil Code section 3533, which has been a statement of policy in this state for 140 years, provides that "The law disregards trifles." The fee request by plaintiff's counsel for $2882.50 on an underlying shortfall of $17.17, when counsel failed to respond to repeated communications from defendant's counsel, is the very type of behavior that gives the honorable profession of being an attorney a bad reputation in the eyes of the non-legal community. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PAUL F. KLAPPER VS. GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief From Privilege Waiver And Order Compelling Production Of Privileged Documents; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED MOTION For Relief From Privilege Waiver And Order Compelling Production Of Privileged Documents. The court adopts the recommendation and reasoning of Temporary Judge Paul Renne in full as follows: Denied. The motion is barred by Code of Civil Procedure section 1008. Although Defendant tries to avoid the language of section 1008 by trying to rely on two other section of the Code of Civil Procedure, it is clear the Gentry is asking the court to set aside the prior order's determination that all objections are waived and the Defendant's failure to file a timely response was due to "excusable neglect." This court is without jurisdiction to grant the type of relief being sought by this Motion. (Kerns v. CSE Ins. Group (2008) 106 Cal.App.4th 368.) As to sanctions, Plaintiff is granted the full amount requested of $3,040.00. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If a court reporter is desired, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO MENDEZ VS. B.A. MOTORS INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 19. DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A INDIANA CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The fraud causes of action are pled with sufficient particularity. The specificity requirement is relaxed where the allegations show that the defendant likely possesses full information concerning the facts of the controversy. Paragraph 43 of the amended complaint alleges that the salesperson was defendant's employee and he can be identified by looking at employee records. Plaintiff pleads justifiable reliance and the fraud exception to the parole evidence rule may apply. The salesperson translated the documents for plaintiff and misrepresented the terms. Plainitff's allegations of concealment are proper even though a fiduciary relationship does not exist. Concealment can be established where there is partial disclosure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 2900 22ND STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. SANTANA REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 26. DEFENDANT ESQUIVAL SANTANA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. There is no authority that a cancelled LLC is different from a dissolved LLC for purposes of being sued. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 2900 22ND STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. SANTANA REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 26. DEFENDANT ESQUIVAL SANTANA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. There is no authority that a cancelled LLC is different from a dissolved LLC for purposes of being sued. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al VS. WILLIAM RALPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 29, 2012, Line 23. DEFENDANT WILLIAM RALPH MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to June 6, 2012 to provide the moving party an opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must provide courtesy copies of the moving and any reply papers to department 302 no later than 4pm May 30 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. If the courtesy copies are not so provided, the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. =(302/HEK)
